data.title: Dentsply Sirona suspends sale of its Byte Aligners and Impression Kits
data.description:
• Dentsply Sirona (NASDAQ:XRAY) announced the voluntary suspension of sales and marketing of its Byte Aligners and Impression Kits while the company conducts a review of certain regulatory requirements related to these products.
  • The company’s decision was made in consultation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
  • In connection with this review, the company has suspended shipment and processing of new and recently placed orders for Byte Aligners and Impression Kits.
  • "Independent of the above matter, the state regulatory environment has adversely impacted our Byte Aligner business model, resulting in declining conversion rates, and new documentation, records and additional requirements (such as evidence of visits to a dentist or patient x-rays)," the company said.
  • The company provides preliminary numbers and expects to report third quarter 2024 net sales of approximately $951M and adjusted EPS is expected to be between $0.49 and $0.51.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NOV GAAP EPS of $0.33 misses by $0.04, revenue of $2.19B misses by $30M
data.description:
• NOV press release (NYSE:NOV): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.33 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $2.19B (flat Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $286 million, an increase of $19 million year-over-year.
  • Cash flow from operations of $359 million and free cash flow of $277 million.
  • Bookings of $627 million, representing a 111% book-to-bill.
  • For the fourth quarter of 2024 management expects year-over-year consolidated revenues to be down three to five percent with Adjusted EBITDA between $280 million and $300 million.
  • Full-year Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be near the lower end of the company's prior guidance range of between $1.10 billion and $1.18 billion.

data.title: Yorbeau Resources chairman acquires 1.2M class A common shares
data.description:
• Yorbeau Resources (TSX:YRB:CA) said its Chairman of the board of directors, Henri Gélinas, has acquired 1.2M class A common shares of the company on October 23.
  • The 1.2M common shares were acquired at a price of C$0.045 per common share and an aggregate purchase price of C$54,000.
  • Immediately following the acquisition, Gélinas had beneficial ownership of, or control or direction over, 46.6M common shares and 500,000 exercisable options to purchase common shares, representing, in the aggregate, about 10.2% of the issued and outstanding common shares of Yorbeau on a partially diluted basis.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Suzano S.A. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Suzano S.A. press release (NYSE:SUZ): Q3 Adjusted EBITDA and Operating cash generation : R$6.5 billion and R$4.4 billion, respectively.
  • Pulp sales of 2,635 thousand tons (6% vs. 3Q23).

data.title: Qualigen Therapeutics announces CEO and CFO retirements
data.description:
• Qualigen Therapeutics (NASDAQ:QLGN) announced that on September 23, 2024, Michael Poirier, notified the board of directors of his intention to resign as Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the Board, effective immediately.
  • On the same date, Christopher Lotz, Chief Financial Officer also tendered his resignation from the company, effective immediately.
  • Both resignations were attributed to disagreements with the company regarding its future direction and strategic initiatives.
  • On September 25, 2024, the board of directors appointed Campbell Becher as President of the Company, effective immediately.
  • On September 26, 2024, the board of directors appointed Kevin Richardson as Interim Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer as well as a member of the board of directors.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Medtronic gets FDA okay for Affera Mapping and Ablation System
data.description:
• Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) has received FDA approval for its Affera Mapping and Ablation System with Sphere-9 Catheter.
  • The system is an all-in-one, high-density mapping and pulsed field and radiofrequency ablation catheter for treatment of persistent atrial fibrillation, or AFib, and for radiofrequency ablation of cavotricuspid isthmus dependent atrial flutter, the company said.
  • Medtronic (MDT) said the approval makes it the only company with two pulsed field ablation, or PFA, technologies available for patients with AFib.
  • The FDA approved Medtronic’s (MDT) PulseSelect Pulsed Field Ablation System in December 2023.
  • Rival Boston Scientific (BSX) received FDA clearance for its FARAPULSE PFA System for the treatment of AFib in January 2024.

data.title: Epsilon Energy forms two joint ventures for Alberta oil exploration
data.description:
Epsilon Energy (NASDAQ:EPSN) +2.6% post-market Thursday after saying it signed agreements to form a joint venture with an unnamed private operator in the Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin in Alberta for a C$12M development carry.

Epsilon (EPSN) said it will earn a 25% working interest in undeveloped lands, covering ~30K gross acres in the Garrington area, targeting horizontal development of the liquids-focused Mannville formation, and 130K gross acres in the Harmattan area, targeting horizontal development of the liquids-focused Upper Viking formation.

Offset wells exhibit attractive economics, the company said, driven by mid-teens effective royalty rates and drilling and completion costs per well estimated at C$600-C$700 per completed lateral ft.

The company also entered a joint venture earlier this year in the Killam area of the Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin in partnership with a separate Calgary-based private operator.

Epsilon (EPSN) said it acquired a 50% working interest in undeveloped lands covering 14K gross acres, also targeting development of the Mannville formation.

data.title: Flushing Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.26 beats by $0.08
data.description:
• Flushing Financial press release (NASDAQ:FFIC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.26 beats by $0.08.
  • Net interest margin FTE decreased 12 bps YoY and increased 5 bps QoQ to 2.10%.

data.title: Rigel issues letter to doctors on fatal infection signal for Gavreto
data.description:
• Rigel Pharmaceuticals has issued a "Dear Healthcare Provider" letter related to a new safety signal associated with Gavreto (pralsetinib), its drug for a type of non-small cell lung cancer.
  • The letter states that an ad hoc analysis of a phase 3 trial "has demonstrated an imbalance regarding the risk of severe and fatal infection, including severe opportunistic infections, between the pralsetinib and [standard of care] arms."
  • The company noted that fatal adverse events were reported in 14 patients (13%) in the pralsetinib arm, compared to 5 patients (4.8%) in the SOC arm. Fatal infections occurred in 5 patients (4.6%) in the pralsetinib arm, and zero in the SOC arm.
  • Rigel says that doctors should monitor patients on the drug for signs and symptoms of infection.

data.title: Charles & Colvard receives non-compliance letter from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Charles & Colvard (NASDAQ:CTHR) on Thursday said it has received a deficiency notification letter from Nasdaq, indicating that the company was not in compliance with listing rules, as a result of its failure to timely file annual report on Form 10-K for the year ended June 30, 2024.
  • The company filed a notification of late filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on October 1, 2024.
  • The notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of the company's common stock on Nasdaq. 
  • Source: Press Release
